message:
Add compaction heuristics to GraphemePool

New constants:
- COMPACTION_FRAGMENTATION_THRESHOLD: 50% fragmentation trigger
- COMPACTION_MIN_SLOTS: Minimum 1000 slots for compaction to be worthwhile

New method should_compact():
- Returns true when both conditions are met
- Prevents unnecessary compaction on small pools
- Prevents compaction when fragmentation is low
- Includes comprehensive doc examples

The heuristic balances memory recovery benefit against compaction overhead.
Small pools or lightly fragmented pools skip compaction since the cost
would exceed the benefit.

Also includes routine beads metadata updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ob","title":"demo_showcase runtime: resilience + capability gating implementation","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Resilience + Capability Gating Implementation

## Purpose

The demo must look great on ideal terminals, but it must also be robust when:

- terminal is too small
- true color is not supported
- hyperlinks are unsupported
- mouse events aren’t available
- stdout/stderr aren’t TTYs

This bead implements the degradation rules defined in `bd-1i7`.

## Behaviors

### Small Terminal

- Define a minimum supported size (from spec).
- Below that:
  - switch to a compact layout (single column or stacked panels)
  - reduce visual density (fewer borders, smaller headers)
  - ensure no panics / out-of-bounds due to rect math

### Missing Capabilities

- If no truecolor: use a reduced palette (256-color-safe or basic).
- If no hyperlinks: render link text without OSC 8 and add a `(copy)` hint.
- If no mouse: hide click hints, ensure keyboard-only navigation covers everything.
- If focus events not available: don’t depend on them for pause behavior.

### Non‑TTY

- Provide a safe fallback:
  - `--headless-smoke` should work without a TTY.
  - If run interactively without TTY and not headless, print a one-line guidance message and exit with non-zero.

## Capability Override (Testability)

To make resilience testable and debuggable, the demo supports an optional capability preset override:

- `--cap-preset <name>` (see `bd-2iv`)

Rules:

- Interactive (TTY): presets can only DISABLE capabilities (effective = detected ∩ preset).
- Headless: presets define the effective caps directly.

The UI should surface effective capabilities (inspector + status hints) so the viewer understands why a feature is disabled.

This is exercised by the degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`).

## Acceptance Notes

- The demo never panics due to resize or missing terminal features.
- UI clearly indicates when features are disabled (subtle warning in status/inspector).
- Degradation behavior is consistent and snapshottable in headless mode.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- The demo never panics due to missing capabilities or resize/relayout.
- Below minimum size, compact layout is activated and remains usable (help/quit always visible).
- When features are disabled (no mouse/links/truecolor/sync), the UI degrades gracefully and indicates the limitation.
- `--cap-preset` only disables capabilities in interactive mode (effective = detected ∩ preset) and defines caps in headless mode.
- The degradation matrix suite (`bd-2bnv`) passes and provides ongoing regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.434807357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497181251Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:34:31.497040558Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1i7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:10.635614919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-1xsw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:32:16.340490585Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:25:49.450381440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.169561512Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-3ii","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:11.747840355Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-ix0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:12.288427025Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ob","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:18.077506237Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ok","title":"demo_showcase spec: visual design system (palette, typography, spacing, components)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Visual Design System

## Goal

A consistent, modern visual language that makes OpenTUI feel premium:

- strong spacing rhythm
- crisp borders
- tasteful gradients
- glassy overlays
- clear focus/selection

This spec defines **multiple themes** because the demo explicitly showcases theme switching (UI + highlighting) as a real app capability.

## Theme Set

### Theme A: “Synthwave Professional” (Default, Dark)

Base background:
- BG0 = #0f1220 (app background)
- BG1 = #151a2e (panel background)
- BG2 = #1d2440 (raised surfaces / cards)

Text:
- FG0 = #e6e6e6 (primary)
- FG1 = #aeb6d6 (secondary)
- FG2 = #6c7396 (dim)

Accents:
- Aqua   = #4dd6ff
- Pink   = #ff4fd8
- Green  = #2bff88
- Orange = #ffb020
- Red    = #ff4455

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #2a335c (typically applied as a low-alpha overlay)
- focus border: Aqua (and bold)

### Theme B: “Light (Paper)”

Base background:
- BG0 = #f7f7fb
- BG1 = #ffffff
- BG2 = #eef0f7

Text:
- FG0 = #1a1b26
- FG1 = #3a3f5a
- FG2 = #6a6f8a

Accents:
- Blue   = #2a6fff
- Purple = #7b61ff
- Green  = #00a86b
- Orange = #ff8a00
- Red    = #e53935

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #dbe6ff (low alpha)
- focus border: Blue

### Theme C: “Solarized-ish” (Low Eye Strain)

Base background:
- BG0 = #002b36
- BG1 = #073642
- BG2 = #0b4452

Text:
- FG0 = #eee8d5
- FG1 = #93a1a1
- FG2 = #657b83

Accents:
- Cyan   = #2aa198
- Yellow = #b58900
- Orange = #cb4b16
- Red    = #dc322f
- Blue   = #268bd2

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #0d5161 (low alpha)
- focus border: Cyan

### Theme D: “High Contrast” (Accessibility / Worst-Case Terminals)

Base background:
- BG0 = #000000
- BG1 = #000000
- BG2 = #111111

Text:
- FG0 = #ffffff
- FG1 = #e0e0e0
- FG2 = #a0a0a0

Accents:
- Cyan   = #00ffff
- Yellow = #ffff00
- Magenta= #ff00ff
- Green  = #00ff00
- Red    = #ff0000

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #333333
- focus border: Yellow

## Component Styling Tokens (Theme-Relative)

- Panel border: dim FG2
- Focused panel border: focus accent + bold + optional 1px “glow line”
- Headers: gradient bar + bold title
- Status bar: BG2 with muted text; hotkeys in FG0 bold
- Toasts: rounded box + opacity stack (glass)

## Typography / Characters

- Prefer Unicode box drawing (rounded/heavy where appropriate).
- Use subtle separators: │, ─, ·.
- Keep iconography mostly to single-codepoint symbols for stable width.

## Gradient Rules

- Gradients should be subtle: 5–10% perceived change across width.
- Use Rgba::lerp between two close hues.

## Theme Switching Rules

- Theme switching changes BOTH:
  1) UI palette/token set
  2) syntax highlighting theme (Theme::dark/light/…)

The demo should present theme switching as a real workflow feature:

- via command palette
- optionally via number keys when help is open (discoverable)

## Degradation Rules (Terminal Capability Variance)

- If no truecolor, map theme colors to the closest 256-color approximation.
- Avoid relying on italics/strikethrough; treat as “bonus” if supported.

## What This Bead Produces

- Canonical theme token sets used everywhere in the demo.
- A single “current theme” selection mechanism shared by chrome, panels, overlays, toasts, and highlighting.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette tokens defined (BG/FG/Accents)
- [ ] Focus/selection styling rules defined
- [ ] Component styling rules defined (borders, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient rules defined
- [ ] Degradation rules defined","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.420443553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358210255Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358144072Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created docs/DEMO_SHOWCASE_DESIGN_SYSTEM.md with 4 theme definitions, component styling, typography, spacing, gradients, and degradation rules","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.450428345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.107964883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pa8","title":"Fix over-permissive terminal capability defaults","description":"## Problem

src/terminal/capabilities.rs lines 59-81 defaults to very permissive capabilities:

```rust
impl Default for Capabilities {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            color: ColorSupport::TrueColor,  // Assumes best case!
            unicode: true,
            mouse: true,
            focus: true,
            bracketed_paste: true,
            // ... all enabled
        }
    }
}
```

## Impact

If capability detection fails (e.g., broken TERM variable, unusual terminal):
- Code assumes TrueColor support
- Sends true color escape sequences to terminal that doesn't support them
- Terminal displays garbage or ignores sequences
- User sees corrupted output

## Better Approach

Default to minimal capabilities and only enable after confirmation:

```rust
impl Default for Capabilities {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            color: ColorSupport::Basic,  // Conservative default
            unicode: false,  // Don't assume
            mouse: false,
            focus: false,
            bracketed_paste: false,
            // ... minimal defaults
        }
    }
}
```

Then in detect():
```rust
pub fn detect() -> Self {
    let mut caps = Self::default();  // Start minimal
    
    // Enable features based on detection
    if has_true_color_env() {
        caps.color = ColorSupport::TrueColor;
    }
    // ...
}
```

## Trade-off

Conservative defaults mean:
- Basic terminals work correctly out of the box
- Advanced terminals get detected features
- But if detection fails, features are disabled (not broken)

## Files to Modify

- src/terminal/capabilities.rs: Default impl, detect() method

## Testing

1. Test with missing TERM variable
2. Test with invalid TERM variable
3. Verify conservative defaults are used","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:03.225484592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:11:03.380028397Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:11:03.379963616Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Changed Default impl to use conservative defaults (Basic color, no unicode, all advanced features disabled). Added is_xterm_compatible() to detect xterm-compatible terminals. detect() now starts conservative and enables features based on TERM value. Updated tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pa8","depends_on_id":"bd-2qib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:11:03.238695079Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pd","title":"demo_showcase UI: implement design system (palette + style helpers + gradients)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Implement Design System

## Purpose

Turn the design system spec (`bd-1ok`) into code inside the demo binary:

- theme token sets (multiple themes)
- color tokens (`Rgba` constants)
- style builders (header style, border style, focus style, selection style)
- gradient helpers using `Rgba::lerp`

## Implementation Pattern

Keep it self-contained inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs`:

- `enum UiTheme { SynthwaveDark, PaperLight, Solarized, HighContrast }`
- `struct Tokens { bg0, bg1, bg2, fg0, fg1, fg2, accent_primary, ... }`
- `impl UiTheme { fn tokens(self, caps: &Capabilities) -> Tokens }`

No file proliferation unless truly necessary.

## Highlight Theme Mapping

Theme switching should update BOTH:

1) UI palette tokens
2) syntax highlighting theme used by the editor panel

Current highlight themes available in the library are:

- Theme::dark()
- Theme::light()
- Theme::high_contrast()

So the mapping should be:

- UiTheme::SynthwaveDark -> Theme::dark()
- UiTheme::PaperLight -> Theme::light()
- UiTheme::Solarized -> Theme::dark() (closest available unless we add a Solarized highlight theme later)
- UiTheme::HighContrast -> Theme::high_contrast()

## Must Demonstrate

- Truecolor gradients when available (still okay if approximated)
- cohesive spacing and hierarchy
- strong focus/selection affordances that remain readable in reduced-color terminals

Dependencies:
- This bead is blocked on the design system spec.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette constants exist and match spec
- [ ] Style helpers exist (borders, focus, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient helper(s) exist and are used in top bar or background","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.059622696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238177379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238002503Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-1ok","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:49.547954895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.071894164Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q0f","title":"EPIC: Event System - Complete API Parity","description":"# Event System Module API Parity

## Zig Reference: Section 15 of EXISTING_OPENTUI_STRUCTURE.md

## Missing Components

### Event Bus
The Zig implementation has a global event dispatcher for terminal events:
- `setEventCallback(fn(name, data))` - Register event handler
- Fires events for terminal responses
- Used for async capability detection

### Logger
Debug logging system with levels:
- `setLogCallback(fn(level, msg))` - Register log handler
- Levels: debug, info, warn, error
- Used throughout the library for diagnostics

## Current Rust State
Need to check if event/logging exists. May need:
- Event trait/callback system
- Log integration (consider tracing crate)
- Terminal response event emission

## Design Considerations
- Should integrate with Rust ecosystem (tracing, log crates)
- Consider async-friendly design
- Event callbacks should be Send + Sync for threading

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Event callback registration
- [ ] Terminal response events
- [ ] Log level filtering
- [ ] Thread-safe event dispatch","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.145501166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.154879861Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1q0f","depends_on_id":"bd-3fx7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:17:15.154828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-271d","title":"Property-based testing suite using proptest","description":"# Property-Based Testing Suite

## Problem
No property-based testing exists in the codebase. Many algorithms handle variable inputs where edge cases could be missed by example-based tests.

## Goals
Add proptest-based testing for algorithms with combinatorial inputs.

## Target Areas

### Color Operations
- [ ] Alpha blending: random colors × opacities → valid output colors
- [ ] HSV conversion: any h,s,v → valid RGB (and round-trip)
- [ ] Color interpolation: lerp preserves bounds
- [ ] 256/16 color conversion: deterministic, reversible where possible

### Text Layout
- [ ] Grapheme iteration: any UTF-8 string → valid graphemes
- [ ] Width calculation: consistent for same string
- [ ] Line wrapping: never exceeds width, preserves content
- [ ] Word boundaries: valid break points

### Buffer Operations
- [ ] Diff algorithm: random changes → valid diff
- [ ] Scissor intersection: always produces valid or empty rect
- [ ] Opacity stacking: product always in [0, 1]

### Input Parsing
- [ ] Valid ANSI sequences: parse → serialize → same output
- [ ] Malformed sequences: never panic, graceful degradation
- [ ] Key events: modifier combinations valid

## Implementation
1. Add proptest to dev-dependencies
2. Create tests/proptests.rs for organized property tests
3. Use proptest::arbitrary for custom generators
4. Target 1000+ test cases per property","notes":"Merged into bd-bh0m (Unit Tests: Property-Based Testing with Proptest). The target areas and goals from this bead have been incorporated with the detailed code examples from bd-bh0m.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:15:16.849666476Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:24:06.754039902Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:24:06.753905181Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-271d","depends_on_id":"bd-pw23","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:15:16.862709468Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27qn","title":"Implement get_cursor_screen_pos() - Cursor screen coordinates","description":"# Task: Implement get_cursor_screen_pos()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn get_cursor_screen_pos(&self) -> (u32, u32)
```

## Purpose
Returns the cursor position in screen coordinates (x, y) relative to the view origin.

## Implementation Notes
1. Get cursor buffer position
2. Calculate visual column (handle tabs, wide chars)
3. Calculate visual row (handle wrapping)
4. Subtract scroll offset

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns correct screen position
- [ ] Handles wide characters
- [ ] Handles tabs
- [ ] Handles wrapping

## Testing
- Cursor at various positions
- With wide characters
- With tabs
- Scrolled view","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:31:21.217983897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:31:21.232027512Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27qn","depends_on_id":"bd-mafj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:31:21.231974313Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-27qz","title":"Audit other OSC-emitting functions for injection","description":"## Context

This task is a follow-up to bd-er8q (Fix C1 control character filtering in set_title()).

## Scope

Audit ALL functions that emit OSC (Operating System Command) sequences to ensure they filter dangerous characters:

### Known OSC-Emitting Functions

1. **set_title()** - Window title (FIXED in bd-er8q)
2. **set_hyperlink()** / hyperlink management
3. **set_clipboard()** if implemented
4. **color palette operations** if implemented
5. **Any custom OSC sequences**

## What to Check

For each OSC-emitting function:
1. Does it accept user-controlled input?
2. Does it filter control characters?
3. Does it filter C1 bytes (0x80-0x9F)?
4. Is there documentation about security considerations?

## Deliverables

1. List of all OSC-emitting functions
2. Assessment of each function's risk level
3. Fixes for any vulnerable functions
4. Security documentation added to each function

## Dependency

Depends on: bd-er8q (the fix pattern to apply)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:10:06.700801473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:44:55.594561912Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:44:55.594498234Z","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: All OSC-emitting functions verified secure.

**Functions audited:**
1. set_title() - Uses char::is_control() filter (C0+C1+DEL)
2. write_hyperlink_start() - Uses escape_url_for_osc8() 
3. escape_url_for_osc8() - Percent-encodes all control chars
4. cursor_color() - Only accepts u8 RGB values, no strings
5. Constants (HYPERLINK_END, CURSOR_COLOR_RESET) - No user input

**Existing test coverage verified:**
- C1 controls (CSI 0x9B, ST 0x9C, OSC 0x9D)
- ESC injection prevention
- BEL/NULL/DEL handling

No vulnerabilities found - codebase is secure against OSC injection.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27qz","depends_on_id":"bd-2mhy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:10:06.719886086Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-27qz","depends_on_id":"bd-er8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:10:16.299094785Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-282m","title":"Implement should_compact() - Compaction heuristic","description":"# Task: Implement should_compact()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn should_compact(&self) -> bool
```

## Purpose
Returns true if compaction would be beneficial based on fragmentation ratio and pool size.

## Heuristic
```rust
// Example heuristic
let ratio = self.get_fragmentation_ratio();
let size = self.total_slots;
ratio > 0.5 && size > 1000  // More than 50% fragmented and significant size
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns reasonable recommendation
- [ ] Considers both ratio and size

## Testing
- Small fragmented pool (false - not worth it)
- Large fragmented pool (true)
- Large unfragmented pool (false)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:58.100412108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:58.109090184Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-282m","depends_on_id":"bd-g3az","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:35:58.109063835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-282m","title":"Implement should_compact() - Compaction heuristic","description":"# Task: Implement should_compact()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn should_compact(&self) -> bool
```

## Purpose
Returns true if compaction would be beneficial based on fragmentation ratio and pool size.

## Heuristic
```rust
// Example heuristic
let ratio = self.get_fragmentation_ratio();
let size = self.total_slots;
ratio > 0.5 && size > 1000  // More than 50% fragmented and significant size
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns reasonable recommendation
- [ ] Considers both ratio and size

## Testing
- Small fragmented pool (false - not worth it)
- Large fragmented pool (true)

 {"id":"bd-28gx","title":"Add GraphemePool size limits and utilization warnings","description":"## Problem

src/grapheme_pool.rs has no safeguards against unbounded growth:

```rust
pub struct GraphemePool {
    slots: Vec<Slot>,  // No size limit!
    free_list: Vec<u32>,
}
```

## Impact

Long-running applications can exhaust memory:
1. Application processes many unique graphemes
2. Graphemes aren't properly decref()'d (leak)
3. Pool grows indefinitely
4. Eventually OOM

## Required Features

### Size Limit
```rust
const DEFAULT_MAX_POOL_SIZE: usize = 1_000_000;  // 1M entries

pub struct GraphemePool {
    slots: Vec<Slot>,
    free_list: Vec<u32>,
    max_size: usize,  // Configurable limit
}

impl GraphemePool {
    pub fn with_max_size(max_size: usize) -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn alloc(&mut self, grapheme: &str) -> Result<GraphemeId, PoolError> {
        if self.slots.len() >= self.max_size && self.free_list.is_empty() {
            return Err(PoolError::PoolFull);
        }
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Utilization Metrics
```rust
impl GraphemePool {
    pub fn utilization(&self) -> PoolStats {
        PoolStats {
            total_slots: self.slots.len(),
            active_slots: self.slots.len() - self.free_list.len(),
            free_slots: self.free_list.len(),
            max_size: self.max_size,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn is_high_utilization(&self) -> bool {
        let active = self.slots.len() - self.free_list.len();
        active > self.max_size * 80 / 100  // 80% threshold
    }
}
```

### Usage Warnings

Consider logging warnings at 80%, 90%, 95% utilization:
```rust
pub fn alloc(&mut self, grapheme: &str) -> GraphemeId {
    let utilization = self.active_count() * 100 / self.max_size;
    if utilization >= 95 && !self.warned_95 {
        log::warn!("GraphemePool at 95% capacity");
        self.warned_95 = true;
    }
    // ...
}
```

## Files to Modify

- src/grapheme_pool.rs: Add max_size, utilization metrics, warnings

## Testing

1. Test pool at capacity
2. Test utilization calculation
3. Verify warnings trigger at thresholds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:08:46.261603815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:13:35.740471237Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:13:35.740408831Z","close_reason":"Added soft_limit, PoolStats, utilization metrics, try_alloc/try_intern with limit checking. 11 new tests. All 33 grapheme_pool tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28gx","depends_on_id":"bd-l4ig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:08:46.270145116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-29z","title":"[EPIC] demo_showcase: runtime (input loop, resize, frame pacing, rendering)","description":"# Epic: demo_showcase — Runtime + Rendering Pipeline

## Why This Needs Its Own Epic

The demo’s *wow factor* will collapse if the runtime stutters, flickers, or breaks on resize.
This epic ensures correctness and smoothness first, so later UI beads can focus on content.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:

 {"id":"bd-2ma8","title":"Implement parse_cursor_position_response() - Parse DSR response","description":"# Task: Implement parse_cursor_position_response()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn parse_cursor_position_response(data: &[u8]) -> Option<(u32, u32)>
```

## Purpose
Parses a Device Status Report (DSR) response for cursor position. Response format: ESC [ row ; col R

## Response Format
- Request: \x1b[6n (DSR - cursor position)
- Response: \x1b[<row>;<col>R
- Row and col are 1-indexed

## Implementation Notes
1. Verify starts with ESC [
2. Parse row number
3. Verify ; separator
4. Parse col number
5. Verify ends with R
6. Convert to 0-indexed

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parses valid response
- [ ] Returns None for invalid
- [ ] Handles multi-digit numbers
- [ ] Converts to 0-indexed

## Testing
- Various valid responses
- Invalid format
- Truncated response","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:05.380118516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:05.396470291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ma8","depends_on_id":"bd-5yy0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:32:05.396412794Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mhy","title":"EPIC: Security Audit - Escape Sequence Injection Prevention","description":"## Overview

This epic addresses a **CRITICAL SECURITY VULNERABILITY** discovered during the deep codebase audit.

## The Vulnerability

**Location:** src/terminal/mod.rs lines 258-277

The set_title() function filters control characters using char::is_control(), which only catches ASCII control characters (0x00-0x1F, 0x7F). However, it FAILS to filter C1 control characters (0x80-0x9F).

### Why This Matters

C1 control characters include:
- CSI (0x9B) - Control Sequence Introducer (equivalent to ESC [)
- OSC (0x9D) - Operating System Command (equivalent to ESC ])
- DCS (0x90) - Device Control String

An attacker who can control the window title could inject these bytes to break out of OSC sequences and inject arbitrary terminal commands.

## Fix Strategy

1. Expand filter to include C1 control bytes (0x80-0x9F)
2. Use explicit whitelist approach instead of blacklist
3. Add regression tests with malicious payloads

## Acceptance Criteria

- C1 bytes (0x80-0x9F) filtered from title strings
- Unit tests verify malicious sequences blocked
- Other OSC-emitting functions audited","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:52.261442580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:09:38.467986882Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:09:38.467923675Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- contains() point-in-rectangle test

- Empty intersection returns None

### Stack Operations
- push() intersects with current
- pop() restores previous
- Stack starts with full buffer bounds
- Nested scissors properly intersect

### Edge Cases
- Zero-width rectangle
- Zero-height rectangle
- Negative coordinates (signed i32)
- Rectangle entirely outside current scissor
- Rectangle partially overlapping
- Very deep stack (100+ levels)
- pop() on base scissor behavior

### Boundary Conditions
- Point exactly on edge
- Point at corners
- Overflow handling in intersection math

### Integration
- is_within_scissor() used by drawing ops
- Scissor affects all drawing operations

## Files to Modify
- src/buffer/scissor.rs - expand tests module

## Acceptance Criteria
- 15+ new tests for scissor stack
- All boundary conditions covered
- Nested clipping thoroughly tested","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:02.874987498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:54.958894481Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:48:54.958817187Z","close_reason":"Added 32 comprehensive scissor stack tests: ClipRect creation/default/isEmpty (6 tests), contains with origin/corners/edges/negative coords (5 tests), intersect full/partial/none/touching/contained/negative/commutative (7 tests), ScissorStack push/pop/clear/nested/deep-nesting-50/disjoint/empty-stack-pop/current/is_empty (14 tests).  {"id":"bd-2mu","title":"demo_showcase spec: app concept + panel layout wireframe","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — App Concept + Panel Layout Wireframe

## App Concept (The “Realistic” Story)

The demo app presents itself as a **Developer Workbench**:

- You’re looking at a project
- You’re editing a file
- You have a live preview/inspector
- You have logs/notifications and quick actions

This is believable, and it naturally uses all OpenTUI primitives.

## Core Screen Layout (Baseline)

Single-screen layout with persistent chrome:

- **Top bar**: brand, mode, time, quick actions (clickable)
- **Sidebar** (left): navigation list (scrollable + clipped)
- **Main editor** (center): `EditorView` (highlighting, selection)
- **Preview** (right): pixel/charts + “compositing” demos
- **Bottom status bar**: key hints + stats
- **Toast area**: transient notifications

### ASCII Wireframe

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  OpenTUI Showcase      Project: opentui_rust      Mode: Normal     22:05   │
├───────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┤
│ Sidebar       │ Editor                                    │ Preview       │
│ (scroll)      │ (rope + undo + highlight)                 │ (pixels)      │
│               │                                           │ (charts)      │
│  • Overview   │  fn main() {                              │   ███░░       │
│  • Editor     │      println!("hello");                   │   ▜▛▌▐        │
│  • Preview    │  }                                        │               │
│  • Logs       │                                           │  Alpha blend  │
│  • Unicode    │                                           │  Scissor clip │
│               │                                           │               │
├───────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────────┴───────────────┤
│  F1 Help  Ctrl+P Command  Ctrl+T Tour  Ctrl+Q Quit   FPS:60  Cells:123     │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Navigation Model (Sidebar “Sections”)

The sidebar is not just focus; it selects a **Section** that drives what content is emphasized.

Suggested model:

- `enum Section { Overview, Editor, Preview, Logs, Unicode, Performance, Settings }`

Rules:

- In the normal (wide) layout, the main panels remain visible, but the selected section:
  - sets a sensible default focus
  - chooses what the preview area shows (pixels vs unicode showpiece vs perf graphs)
  - can change the status bar hints and the inspector emphasis

Example mapping (initial):

- Overview: default "wow" composition (pixels + logs alive + editor visible)
- Unicode: preview area becomes the grapheme pool showpiece + width rulers
- Performance: preview area becomes perf charts; inspector is pinned/expanded
- Settings: opens a settings overlay or uses the command palette as the primary UI

In compact layout (small terminals), the selected section chooses which panel gets the full-screen treatment.

## Overlay Screens

Overlays (semi-transparent, “glass”):

- **Command palette** (Ctrl+P): fuzzy-ish list + preview
- **Help overlay** (F1): keybinds + feature legend
- **Tour overlay** (Ctrl+T): step title + next action

Overlays should use:
- `push_opacity()` + blended background rectangles
- scissor clipping for internal list scroll

## Layout Rules

- Everything is manual rectangles (OpenTUI is not a layout engine).
- Each panel computes a `Rect { x,y,w,h }`.
- Panels are clipped to their rect via scissor stack.
- Borders/headers are drawn with box styles + subtle gradients.

## What This Bead Produces

- A stable “screen map” other beads can implement against.
- A clear definition of Sections vs Focus, so navigation and tour scripts remain coherent.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Baseline layout defined (top/sidebar/editor/preview/logs/status)
- [ ] Overlays defined (help, command palette, tour)
- [ ] Each region has explicit responsibilities + feature coverage
- [ ] Layout rules for clipping/scrolling are specified","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:14.680066085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:12:21.119864269Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:12:21.119772458Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created docs/DEMO_SHOWCASE_SPEC.md with full layout wireframe, panel specifications, overlay definitions, navigation model, and layout rules","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mu","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:14.692986102Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
